title: Point Reyes Light — February 14, 1963 (Page 1)
author: Point Reyes Light Staff
card_summary: A single front page from the Point Reyes Light, a weekly community newspaper serving Point Reyes Station, California, dated February 14, 1963. Philip K. Dick resided in the Point Reyes area during this period, and issues from this paper are preserved as contextual documents relating to his life and community.
page_summary:
A scanned front page from the Point Reyes Light (Point Reyes Station, California), dated Thursday, February 14, 1963. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 5, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

As a front-page clipping from early 1963, this item dates from a period when Dick was still living in the Marin County area. The Man in the High Castle had been published the previous year and would win the Hugo Award in September 1963. This was also a period of increasing personal turbulence for Dick, as his marriage to Anne Dick was beginning to deteriorate. The Point Reyes Light covered local news in the rural western Marin communities near Dick's residence.
